journal_entry.reason: Ford discounted a non-interest-bearing note at the bank with derecognition. The note has a face value of $100,000 and 40 days remaining to maturity (90 - 50 = 40 days). The bank charges a discount of $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Ford receives net proceeds of $98,356.16 in cash. Since management determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9, Ford removes the note receivable from its books and recognizes the difference between the carrying amount ($100,000) and net proceeds ($98,356.16) as a loss on discounting.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Ford received a 90-day non-interest-bearing promissory note with face value $100,000 as settlement of a trade receivable. After 50 days, Ford discounted the note at the bank at 15% annual discount rate. The note has 40 days remaining to maturity (90 - 50 = 40). Using actual/365 day-count convention, the discount charge is $100,000 × 0.15 × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds received in cash are $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Management has determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9, meaning Ford transfers the risks and rewards of the note to the bank and removes it from the books. The difference between the carrying amount of the note ($100,000) and the net proceeds ($98,356.16) is recognized as a loss on discounting ($1,643.84). No tax is mentioned in the transaction.
